content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify verified, genuine defects in the timeout enforcement and retry infrastructure. The code confirms: (1) OpenRouter relies on reqwest's timeout which doesn't guarantee wall-clock enforcement against slow-streaming responses, yet claims enforces_timeout()=true; (2) TmuxAdapter delegates enforces_timeout() to ProcessBackendAdapter (returning true) but its invoke() never applies tokio::time::timeout — wait_for_session_exit() is an unbounded polling loop; (3) Missing OPENROUTER_API_KEY produces BackendUnavailable with failure_class: None, which maps to TransportFailure and triggers 5 retries for a deterministic configuration error.

## Votes

- `fr-7-f5efd49b`: Accept
  Verified: OpenRouterBackendAdapter returns enforces_timeout() -> true (line 359) and sets timeout via reqwest::Client::builder().timeout() (line 105). Unlike ProcessBackendAdapter which wraps in tokio::time::timeout (line 1045) giving a hard wall-clock guarantee, reqwest's timeout may not fire if the server trickle-streams data (bytes keep arriving, preventing the timeout from triggering). The service adds a 30-second buffer (service.rs lines 204-213) under the premise that the adapter fires first, but for slow-streaming responses the adapter may never fire, delaying cancellation by 30 seconds. Either returning false or wrapping in tokio::time::timeout are sound fixes.
- `fr-7-144385ef`: Accept
  Verified: TmuxAdapter.enforces_timeout() delegates to self.process.enforces_timeout() (tmux.rs line 453), which returns true. However, TmuxAdapter.invoke() (lines 471-595) never applies tokio::time::timeout. It calls wait_for_session_exit() (line 519) which is an unbounded polling loop (lines 342-382) with no timeout mechanism whatsoever. The ProcessBackendAdapter's own tokio::time::timeout in spawn_and_wait() (line 1045) is never invoked because TmuxAdapter bypasses the process adapter's invoke(). The 30-second service buffer is therefore granted under a false premise, causing tmux runs to exceed the configured timeout by ~30 seconds before cancellation.
- `fr-7-9872eaf7`: Accept
  Verified: api_key() (openrouter_backend.rs lines 64-76) returns AppError::BackendUnavailable with failure_class: None when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is unset. AppError::failure_class() (error.rs line 311) maps None to FailureClass::TransportFailure via unwrap_or. RetryPolicy (retry_policy.rs line 45) retries TransportFailure up to 5 times with exponential backoff. A missing environment variable is a deterministic prerequisite failure that will never succeed on retry — this wastes up to ~2.5 minutes of backoff time and obscures the root cause. Setting a terminal failure_class (or adding a dedicated prerequisite error variant) would fail immediately and surface a clear error message.
